summary: Implemented the M5 Dashboard and Opportunity List UI slice. Added server-rendered /dashboard and /opportunities pages, shared Prisma-backed opportunity view-model helpers, reusable OpportunityCard and ScoreBadge components, query-string filters, focused tests, and README documentation. The lead also corrected dashboard metrics so persisted database counts/average scores are not truncated by the visible Top-window query.

tests_run.result: PASS: 6 focused tests passed for Dashboard Top 5 ordering, persisted-count dashboard metrics, query filter normalization/application, serializable view models, empty states, ScoreBadge rendering, and OpportunityCard risk/monetization/detail-link rendering.
PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully. Nonfatal local Windows SWC native-package warnings were printed before fallback build continued.
PASS: no new whitespace errors. Git printed the existing README.md line-ending conversion warning; README lines 3-4 intentionally retain Markdown hard-break spaces.

decisions: Kept data access in lib/opportunities.ts and used dynamic import of '@/lib/db' inside server-side helper functions so UI components receive plain serializable props and tests can import pure builders without pulling Prisma runtime state.

Implemented /dashboard as a server-rendered operational page with today's summary metrics, today's Top 5 opportunities sorted by totalScore descending, and recent scan runs from persisted SearchRun rows.
Implemented /opportunities as a score-sorted server-rendered list with query-string filters for radarTaskId/task, minScore, toolType, riskLevel/risk, and status.
Derived risk level from stored rawAnalysis, riskSummary, and scoreBreakdown.riskPenalty because this slice does not add a dedicated risk column or schema migration.
Rendered risk badges on every OpportunityCard, including medium/high/excluded/unknown treatments, instead of hiding risky opportunities in this list slice.

Added bounded Prisma queries for dashboard opportunities, recent runs, opportunity list rows, and filter option rows; dashboard count and average score metrics use persisted aggregate/count results so the visible query window does not distort summary metrics.
Rendered stable links to /opportunities/{id} as detail targets while intentionally not implementing the detail route or status mutation behavior reserved for later tasks.

Kept styling restrained and consistent with the existing Tailwind palette, with no marketing hero, global style changes, decorative backgrounds, nested cards, package changes, API changes, or schema changes.
risks: The /opportunities list is intentionally bounded for local operator use. Risk filtering is applied after serialization because risk is derived from JSON/text fields, so a very large local dataset may need narrower filters or a later dedicated risk column.
Opportunity detail links intentionally point to a future route. This satisfies the task's detail-link target requirement but clicking them will 404 until the separate detail task is implemented.
Dashboard risk average is a cue across the visible top opportunity window, not a full-dataset risk aggregate, because risk is derived rather than stored as an indexed scalar in this slice.
The dashboard is scoped to today's local-day rows; historical/global analytics remain out of scope for this slice.
requested_action: Review M5-DASHBOARD-OPPORTUNITY-LIST-001 against architecture/data-boundary requirements, then route to domain_designer and qa_reviewer for independent acceptance.
